---
title: "HF Internal Branch Length Analysis"
format: html
jupyter: julia-1.10
---

```{julia}
using PhyloNetworks 
using CSV, DataFrames
using PhyloPlots

# Include utility functions 
current_dir = pwd() 
println("current_dir: $current_dir")

root_dir = joinpath(current_dir, "..", "..")     
println("root_dir: $root_dir") 

utility_path = joinpath(root_dir, "scripts", "utilities.jl") 
include(utility_path)
```

### We want to calculate the average internal branch length for each locus tree 
This is how we generate the example data: 

```
julia -p 10 scripts/simulation.jl --dup_rate 1e-4 --loss_rate 1e-4 --ratevar L --n_inds 1 --SF 1 --n_genes 10 --n_reps 10 
```

```{julia}
# set up the root name for parameter files from utilities.jl
data_dir = joinpath(root_dir, "output", paramname_root)
paramname_root = set_up_paramname_root(1e-4, 1e-4, "L", 1, 1.0) # set up the root name for parameter files from utilities.jl
data_dir = joinpath(root_dir, "output", paramname_root)
check_file = joinpath(data_dir, "genetrees_taxa_and_internal_branch_DUP0.0001-LOS0.0001-RVL-N_ind1-SF1.0.csv")

# read CSV into a DataFrame
if isfile(check_file)
    df_stats = CSV.read(check_file, DataFrame)
    first(df_stats, min(10, nrow(df_stats)))
end 
```

We want to check if the results generated from our pipeline is accurate. Let's check for rep 01 gene trees (first sample and the 10th sample). Three things needed to be checked: 
    1. Is the number of taxa shown in the result file correct? 
    2. Is the internal branch length for locus tree calculated correct? 
    3. Is the internal branch length for gene tree calculated corrtec? 

```{julia}
# Get the first tree: 
ex1_str = "rep01/genetrees_singlecopy/g_trees_noLocusID_Gene08_Int1.trees"
gene_tree_file_ex1 = joinpath(data_dir, ex1_str)
gene_tree_ex1 = readnewick(gene_tree_file_ex1) 
plot(gene_tree_ex1, useedgelength=true)

# find locus tree 
locus_tree_ex1 = readnewick(readlines(joinpath(data_dir, 
                        "rep01/genetrees_simphy/Int1/1/l_trees.trees"))[8]) 


# remove the lost tips first from locus tree 
to_remove = [t for t in tiplabels(locus_tree_ex1) if startswith(t, "Lost")]
for tip in to_remove # only locus tree needs to remove lost tips
    deleteleaf!(locus_tree_ex1, tip)
end 
plot(locus_tree_ex1, useedgelength=true)  

# print out the number of taxa 
println("Number of taxa in locus tree: $(length(tiplabels(locus_tree_ex1)))")
println("Number of taxa in gene tree: $(length(tiplabels(gene_tree_ex1)))")  
```

```{julia}
function calculate_internal_branch_lengths(locus_tree::PhyloNetworks.HybridNetwork, 
                                        gene_tree::PhyloNetworks.HybridNetwork)
    # calculate internal branch lengths for locus tree 
    internal_branch_lengths_per_locus_tree = Float64[] 
    # calculate internal branch lengths for gene tree 
    internal_branch_lengths_per_gene_tree = Float64[] 

    removedegree2nodes!(locus_tree) # remove degree 2 nodes first  
    removedegree2nodes!(gene_tree) # remove degree 2 nodes first 

    for edge in locus_tree.edge # internal branch lengths for locus tree 
        if !isexternal(edge) 
            push!(internal_branch_lengths_per_locus_tree, edge.length)
        end     
    end
    for edge in gene_tree.edge # internal branch lengths for gene tree 
        if !isexternal(edge) 
            push!(internal_branch_lengths_per_gene_tree, edge.length)
        end
    end
    avg_locus_branch_length = mean(internal_branch_lengths_per_locus_tree)
    avg_gene_branch_length = mean(internal_branch_lengths_per_gene_tree) 
    return avg_locus_branch_length, avg_gene_branch_length 
end
```

```{julia}
avg_locus_branch_length, avg_gene_branch_length = calculate_internal_branch_lengths(locus_tree_ex1, gene_tree_ex1) 
println("Average internal branch length for locus tree: $avg_locus_branch_length")
println("Average internal branch length for gene tree: $avg_gene_branch_length") 
```

The above calculation is consistent with our results from the pipeline. 

```{julia}
# Get the first tree: 
ex2_str = "rep01/genetrees_singlecopy/g_trees_noLocusID_Gene44_Int1.trees"
gene_tree_file_ex2 = joinpath(data_dir, ex2_str)
gene_tree_ex2 = readnewick(gene_tree_file_ex2) 
plot(gene_tree_ex2, useedgelength=true)

# find locus tree 
locus_tree_ex2 = readnewick(readlines(joinpath(data_dir, 
                        "rep01/genetrees_simphy/Int1/1/l_trees.trees"))[44]) 

# remove the lost tips first from locus tree 
to_remove = [t for t in tiplabels(locus_tree_ex2) if startswith(t, "Lost")]
for tip in to_remove # only locus tree needs to remove lost tips
    deleteleaf!(locus_tree_ex2, tip)
end 
plot(locus_tree_ex2, useedgelength=true)  

# print out the number of taxa 
println("Number of taxa in locus tree: $(length(tiplabels(locus_tree_ex2)))")
println("Number of taxa in gene tree: $(length(tiplabels(gene_tree_ex2)))")  
```

```{julia}
avg_locus_branch_length_2, avg_gene_branch_length_2 = calculate_internal_branch_lengths(locus_tree_ex2, gene_tree_ex2) 
println("Average internal branch length for locus tree: $avg_locus_branch_length_2")
println("Average internal branch length for gene tree: $avg_gene_branch_length_2") 
```

The second example also corresponds with the results from our pipeline.
